External threats intensified as migratory groups breached fortified frontiers along the Rhine and Danube. Political instability fractured the administrative center, rendering the city unable to project power effectively. The aftermath established numerous successor kingdoms and initiated the shift toward medieval governance structures.<|im_end|>
